status_policy.PASS: The assigned check succeeds under its declared assumptions.
status_policy.CONDITIONAL: Additional mathematical, model, regularity, or empirical assumptions remain required.
status_policy.DEFINITION: The object is a definition, ansatz, or bookkeeping object rather than a theorem.
status_policy.OPEN: The assigned proof, derivation, simulation, or empirical test remains unresolved.
status_policy.FAIL: The encoded statement is contradicted by its assigned checker.

assumptions.name: Compatible loop orientation
assumptions.statement: The winding orientation and the curvature orientation use the same sign convention.

assumptions.name: Exact integrated phase-curvature locking
assumptions.statement: The integrated phase winding equals the integrated curvature contribution without a residual mismatch term.

assumptions.name: Constant positive loop weight
assumptions.statement: The loop weight is positive and constant, so weighted and unweighted loop averages coincide.

assumptions.name: Positive spectral offset
assumptions.statement: The Green-kernel denominator contains a strictly positive offset, preventing a pole on the declared domain.

assumptions.name: Standard Sobolev domain hypotheses
assumptions.statement: The spatial domain satisfies the regularity and extension hypotheses required by the stated Sobolev embedding.

assumptions.name: H2 confinement route
assumptions.statement: The declared confinement and stability argument requires control through the H2-to-L-infinity regularity route.

claims.title: Curvature-derived mass

papers.title: The Geometry of Resonance: Wave Confinement Theory and the Emergence of Mass, Force, and Spacetime
Emergence of Effective Mass: Solenoidal Topology of Vibrational Energy
Rest Energy from Density-Weighted Loop Curvature: A Covariant Locking Principle
Logarithmic Curvature Flow, Filament Localization, and the Geometric Origin of the Lepton Mass Spectrum

evidence.description: Exact loop closure plus constant positive weight closes the registered effective-wavenumber algebraic chain; it does not establish dynamical realization or physical mass generation.
Lean kernel checks the finite algebraic E5 chain under explicit closure and weighting hypotheses.

falsifiers: A calibrated system satisfying the declared locking assumptions does not produce the predicted effective mass relation within stated uncertainty.
The required curvature scale cannot be selected dynamically without fitting the target mass.
unresolved_obligations: Establish that the dynamically selected curvature scale determines physical mass rather than merely supplying a dimensionally valid mapping.
Close or calibrate the model-dependent proportionality in E19.
Publish a parameter-frozen empirical test connecting measured curvature or spectral scale to inertial mass.

claims.title: Finite-band pattern selection

papers.title: Phase-Flux Field (PFF): Axiomatic Substrate for Wave Confinement Theory
Self-Emergent Fourier Cymatics: Entropic Eigenmodes out of Chaos
The Geometry of Resonance: Wave Confinement Theory and the Emergence of Mass, Force, and Spacetime

evidence.description: Executable checks verify the preferred-shell extremum, sign convention, projection identities, selected wavelength, and bounded Green kernel under declared assumptions.
Lean checks the one-mode polynomial symbol and Green-kernel inequalities, not the complete nonlinear pattern-selection theorem.
Author-generated numerical studies report finite-band spectral concentration for selected models and parameters.

falsifiers: With frozen equations, discretization, parameters, and initial-condition ensemble, the predicted preferred shell fails to appear or is not robust to resolution and domain changes.
The measured selected wavelength disagrees with the declared dispersion maximum after uncertainty propagation.
unresolved_obligations: Distinguish linear spectral selection from nonlinear global pattern selection.
Connect the selected shell to calibrated experimental observables.
Publish a frozen cross-resolution and cross-integrator reproduction bundle.

claims.title: Spatial-dimensionality stability criterion

papers.title: Hard Upper Bound on Spatial Dimensionality in Wave Confinement Theory
The Geometry of Resonance: Wave Confinement Theory and the Emergence of Mass, Force, and Spacetime

evidence.description: The registered mathematical result is the standard H2-to-L-infinity threshold for integer dimension n at most 3 under standard domain hypotheses.
E70 records the broader WCT stability interpretation as conditional on the H2 confinement route being necessary.

claims.qualification: The verified Sobolev threshold is not a universal biconditional theorem excluding every possible stable higher-dimensional confinement mechanism.
falsifiers: A valid stable WCT confinement mechanism in dimension n greater than or equal to 4 that does not require the declared H2-to-L-infinity route falsifies the universal interpretation while leaving the Sobolev theorem intact.
unresolved_obligations: Prove that the H2 regularity route is necessary for every admissible stable WCT confinement mechanism, or retain explicitly conditional wording.

claims.title: Koide mass relation from curvature-harmonic structure

papers.title: Wave Confinement Theory Predicts the Koide Mass Relation: A Curvature-Harmonic Origin of Fermion Mass Triplets
Logarithmic Curvature Flow, Filament Localization, and the Geometric Origin of the Lepton Mass Spectrum
Rest Energy from Density-Weighted Loop Curvature: A Covariant Locking Principle

evidence.description: Lean establishes positivity and well-definedness of the Koide expression on the positive-mass domain; it does not derive Q = 2/3.
Author-generated numerical and symbolic checks evaluate the relation but do not resolve whether the target value is derived rather than selected or fitted.

falsifiers: A preregistered parameter-free derivation fails to return Q = 2/3 for the declared state-selection rule.
The result changes materially under admissible normalization or unreported discrete model choices.
The target value is found to be imposed as an input rather than produced by the dynamics or geometry.
unresolved_obligations: Determine whether Q = 2/3 is an output or an imposed constraint.
Record which model choices are fixed before evaluating the ratio.
Count available continuous and discrete choices.
Determine whether the selected harmonic state is unique.
Determine dependence on normalization and state selection.
Classify the result as a scale-free phenomenological derivation, fitted relation, or dynamical prediction.

claims.title: Curvature-bounded computation

papers.title: P vs NP in Curvature-Bounded Wave Computation: A Model-Relative P_WCC != NP_WCC Separation
Discrete Wave-Constrained Computation and Classical Complexity: Turing Equivalence for P and NP
The Classical P vs NP Problem Is Mathematically and Physically Ill-Posed
WaveLock: A Curvature-Locked One-Way Function Based on Nonlinear PDE Evolution

evidence.description: The registry checks dimensional and counting relations but leaves encoding, precision, update-cost, and equivalence assumptions open.
WaveLock is an author-generated cryptographic research prototype with unresolved security proof obligations; it is not evidence for unrestricted classical P versus NP.

claims.qualification: All separation and equivalence statements are internal to explicitly defined computational models and must not be reported as an unrestricted solution to classical P versus NP.
falsifiers: A polynomial-overhead simulation invalidates the claimed model-relative separation under the same encoding, precision, and resource assumptions.
The declared curvature-resource restriction cannot be implemented or measured without exponential hidden overhead.
unresolved_obligations: Specify the encoding, precision model, update cost, and finite curvature-resource bound.
Separate physical-resource claims from classical complexity-class equivalence claims.
Provide theorem-level simulation relations between the WCC model and a standard computational model.

objects.name: Curvature-locking functional
objects.formula:
\sigma(s):=\sqrt{\kappa(s)^2+\tau(s)^2}, \qquad \langle f\rangle_w:= \frac{\oint_\Gamma w f\,ds}{\oint_\Gamma w\,ds}, \quad \oint_\Gamma w\,ds>0.

S_{\rm lock}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(s)\bigl(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma\bigr)^2\,ds.

m=\frac{\hbar}{c}\langle\sigma\rangle_w.

objects.name: Failure of $H^2 o L^\infty$ above three dimensions
objects.family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
objects.definition: Current effective status: ✅ PASS For $n>3$, the embedding $H^2\hookrightarrow L^\infty$ fails in general; equivalently, there exist $H^2$ functions not controlled in $L^\infty$.

verification.meaning: The general H2-to-L-infinity embedding route fails above three spatial dimensions under the declared Sobolev framework.
limitations: Failure of this regularity route does not exclude every conceivable higher-dimensional confinement mechanism.
Arithmetic equivalence is proved; embedding is an explicit contract assumption.

objects.name: Dimensional stability criterion
objects.family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
objects.definition: Current effective status: ⚠️ CONDITIONAL Use the one-way criterion

verification.meaning: The Sobolev threshold supports the declared three-dimensional regularity route; the universal WCT stability conclusion remains conditional.
limitations: This is not a biconditional characterization of every stable WCT solution.
Failure of the H2 embedding route above three dimensions is not by itself a universal impossibility theorem.
The criterion includes the dimensional conclusion as a hypothesis; this is not an independent stability theorem.
